// controllers/patientController.go
// Pattern: struct controller + constructor injection + Swagger annotations
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/controllers/
package controllers

import (
	"net/http"
	"time"

	"medical-sas-api/services/contracts"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// PatientController handles HTTP requests for the Patient resource.
type PatientController struct {
	PatientService contracts.PatientServiceContract
}

// NewPatientController constructs a PatientController with its service dependency.
func NewPatientController(svc contracts.PatientServiceContract) *PatientController {
	return &PatientController{PatientService: svc}
}

// CreatePatient godoc
// @Summary     Create a new patient
// @Description Create a new patient in the system
// @Tags        Patients
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       body body contracts.PatientInputDTO true "Patient data"
// @Success     201  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     400  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     422  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /patients [post]
func (ctrl *PatientController) CreatePatient(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var input contracts.PatientInputDTO
	if err := c.BodyParser(&input); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid request payload"})
	}

	if input.Name == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "name is required"})
	}
	if input.Contact == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "contact is required"})
	}

	id, err := ctrl.PatientService.CreateFromInput(input)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusCreated).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"message": "Patient created successfully",
		"id":      id,
	})
}

// GetByCPF godoc
// @Summary     Get patient by CPF
// @Description Retrieve a single patient identified by its CPF
// @Tags        Patients
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       cpf path string true "Patient CPF"
// @Success     200 {object} contracts.PatientDTO
// @Failure     400 {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     404 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /patients/{cpf} [get]
func (ctrl *PatientController) GetByCPF(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	identifier := c.Params("cpf")
	if identifier == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "cpf is required"})
	}

	result, err := ctrl.PatientService.FindByCPF(identifier)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusNotFound).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(result)
}

// ListPatients godoc
// @Summary     List all patients
// @Description Returns a paginated list of patients
// @Tags        Patients
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       page  query int false "Page number (default 1)"
// @Param       limit query int false "Page size (default 20)"
// @Success     200  {array}  contracts.PatientDTO
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /patients [get]
func (ctrl *PatientController) ListPatients(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	page  := c.QueryInt("page", 1)
	limit := c.QueryInt("limit", 20)
	if page < 1  { page = 1 }
	if limit < 1 { limit = 20 }

	results, err := ctrl.PatientService.List(page, limit)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to retrieve patients"})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"data":  results,
		"page":  page,
		"limit": limit,
	})
}

<go_pkg> main

// cmd/main.go — Fiber router setup
// Pattern: group-based routing + auth middleware + Swagger protection
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/cmd/main.go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"

	"medical-sas-api/controllers"
	"medical-sas-api/initializers"
	_ "medical-sas-api/docs"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/basicauth"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/cors"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/logger"
	"github.com/gofiber/swagger"
	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
)

// bearerAuthMiddleware validates the Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
// Returns 401 if missing or invalid; calls Next() on success.
func bearerAuthMiddleware(svc *initializers.Services) fiber.Handler {
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		header := c.Get("Authorization")
		if len(header) <= 7 || header[:7] != "Bearer " {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "missing or malformed Authorization header",
			})
		}
		if err := svc.AuthTokenService.ValidateToken(header[7:]); err != nil {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error":  "invalid or expired token",
				"reason": err.Error(),
			})
		}
		return c.Next()
	}
}

func configureMiddleware(app *fiber.App) {
	app.Use(cors.New(cors.Config{
		AllowOrigins: "*",
		AllowMethods: "GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE,OPTIONS",
		AllowHeaders: "Origin, Content-Type, Accept, Authorization",
	}))
	app.Use(logger.New(logger.Config{
		Format: `[$\{time\}] $\{status\} - $\{method\} $\{path\}\n`,
	}))
}

func registerRoutes(app *fiber.App, svc *initializers.Services) {
	auth := app.Group("/auth")
	auth.Post("/token",    controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).GenerateToken)
	auth.Post("/validate", controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).ValidateToken)

	protected := app.Group("/", bearerAuthMiddleware(svc))

	entityCtrl := controllers.NewPatientController(svc.PatientService)
	patients := protected.Group("/patients")
	patients.Post("/",              entityCtrl.CreatePatient)
	patients.Get("/",               entityCtrl.ListPatients)
	patients.Get("/:cpf",   entityCtrl.GetByCPF)
}

func main() {
	if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil {
		log.Println("warning: .env file not found, using environment")
	}

	db  := initializers.InitialDB()
	initializers.RunMigrations(db)
	svc := initializers.InitServices(db)

	app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{AppName: "medical-sas-api v1"})
	configureMiddleware(app)

	// Swagger endpoint protected by basic auth
	swaggerUser := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_USER")
	swaggerPass := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_PASSWORD")
	if swaggerUser == "" || swaggerPass == "" {
		log.Fatal("SWAGGER_USER and SWAGGER_PASSWORD must be set")
	}
	swaggerAuth := basicauth.New(basicauth.Config{
		Users: map[string]string{swaggerUser: swaggerPass},
	})
	app.Get("/swagger/*", swaggerAuth, swagger.HandlerDefault)

	registerRoutes(app, svc)

	port := os.Getenv("PORT")
	if port == "" {
		port = "8080"
	}
	log.Printf("server listening on :%s", port)
	if err := app.Listen(":" + port); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("server error: %v", err)
	}
}

// cmd/main.go — Fiber router setup
// Pattern: group-based routing + auth middleware + Swagger protection
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/cmd/main.go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"

	"medical-sas-api/controllers"
	"medical-sas-api/initializers"
	_ "medical-sas-api/docs"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/basicauth"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/cors"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/logger"
	"github.com/gofiber/swagger"
	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
)

// bearerAuthMiddleware validates the Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
// Returns 401 if missing or invalid; calls Next() on success.
func bearerAuthMiddleware(svc *initializers.Services) fiber.Handler {
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		header := c.Get("Authorization")
		if len(header) <= 7 || header[:7] != "Bearer " {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "missing or malformed Authorization header",
			})
		}
		if err := svc.AuthTokenService.ValidateToken(header[7:]); err != nil {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error":  "invalid or expired token",
				"reason": err.Error(),
			})
		}
		return c.Next()
	}
}

func configureMiddleware(app *fiber.App) {
	app.Use(cors.New(cors.Config{
		AllowOrigins: "*",
		AllowMethods: "GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE,OPTIONS",
		AllowHeaders: "Origin, Content-Type, Accept, Authorization",
	}))
	app.Use(logger.New(logger.Config{
		Format: `[$\{time\}] $\{status\} - $\{method\} $\{path\}\n`,
	}))
}

func registerRoutes(app *fiber.App, svc *initializers.Services) {
	auth := app.Group("/auth")
	auth.Post("/token",    controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).GenerateToken)
	auth.Post("/validate", controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).ValidateToken)

	protected := app.Group("/", bearerAuthMiddleware(svc))

	entityCtrl := controllers.NewPatientController(svc.PatientService)
	patients := protected.Group("/patients")
	patients.Post("/",              entityCtrl.CreatePatient)
	patients.Get("/",               entityCtrl.ListPatients)
	patients.Get("/:cpf",   entityCtrl.GetByCPF)
}

func main() {
	if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil {
		log.Println("warning: .env file not found, using environment")
	}

	db  := initializers.InitialDB()
	initializers.RunMigrations(db)
	svc := initializers.InitServices(db)

	app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{AppName: "medical-sas-api v1"})
	configureMiddleware(app)

	// Swagger endpoint protected by basic auth
	swaggerUser := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_USER")
	swaggerPass := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_PASSWORD")
	if swaggerUser == "" || swaggerPass == "" {
		log.Fatal("SWAGGER_USER and SWAGGER_PASSWORD must be set")
	}
	swaggerAuth := basicauth.New(basicauth.Config{
		Users: map[string]string{swaggerUser: swaggerPass},
	})
	app.Get("/swagger/*", swaggerAuth, swagger.HandlerDefault)

	registerRoutes(app, svc)

	port := os.Getenv("PORT")
	if port == "" {
		port = "8080"
	}
	log.Printf("server listening on :%s", port)
	if err := app.Listen(":" + port); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("server error: %v", err)
	}
}

// utils/responseUtil.go
// Pattern: response utility interface + JSON helper
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/utils/responseUtil.go
package utils

import (
	"encoding/json"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// ResponseUtil abstracts JSON response construction for testability.
type ResponseUtil interface {
	RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error
	RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error
}

// JSONResponseUtil implements ResponseUtil using Fiber's context.
type JSONResponseUtil struct{}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error {
	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).
			JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to marshal response"})
	}
	c.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	return c.Status(code).Send(body)
}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error {
	return c.Status(code).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": message})
}
</go_file>

// OrderController handles HTTP requests for the Order resource.
type OrderController struct {
	OrderService contracts.OrderServiceContract
}

// NewOrderController constructs a OrderController with its service dependency.
func NewOrderController(svc contracts.OrderServiceContract) *OrderController {
	return &OrderController{OrderService: svc}
}

// CreateOrder godoc
// @Summary     Create a new order
// @Description Create a new order in the system
// @Tags        Orders
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       body body contracts.OrderInputDTO true "Order data"
// @Success     201  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     400  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     422  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /orders [post]

	var input contracts.OrderInputDTO
	if err := c.BodyParser(&input); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid request payload"})
	}

	if input.CustomerID == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "customer_id is required"})
	}
	if len(input.Items) == 0 {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "items cannot be empty"})
	}

	id, err := ctrl.OrderService.CreateFromInput(input)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusCreated).JSON(fiber.Map{

}

// cmd/main.go — Fiber router setup
// Pattern: group-based routing + auth middleware + Swagger protection
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/cmd/main.go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"

	"ecommerce-api/controllers"
	"ecommerce-api/initializers"
	_ "ecommerce-api/docs"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/basicauth"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/cors"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/logger"
	"github.com/gofiber/swagger"
	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
)

// bearerAuthMiddleware validates the Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
// Returns 401 if missing or invalid; calls Next() on success.
func bearerAuthMiddleware(svc *initializers.Services) fiber.Handler {
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		header := c.Get("Authorization")
		if len(header) <= 7 || header[:7] != "Bearer " {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "missing or malformed Authorization header",
			})
		}
		if err := svc.AuthTokenService.ValidateToken(header[7:]); err != nil {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error":  "invalid or expired token",
				"reason": err.Error(),
			})
		}
		return c.Next()
	}
}

func configureMiddleware(app *fiber.App) {
	app.Use(cors.New(cors.Config{
		AllowOrigins: "*",
		AllowMethods: "GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE,OPTIONS",
		AllowHeaders: "Origin, Content-Type, Accept, Authorization",
	}))
	app.Use(logger.New(logger.Config{
		Format: `[$\{time\}] $\{status\} - $\{method\} $\{path\}\n`,
	}))
}

func registerRoutes(app *fiber.App, svc *initializers.Services) {
	auth := app.Group("/auth")
	auth.Post("/token",    controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).GenerateToken)
	auth.Post("/validate", controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).ValidateToken)

	protected := app.Group("/", bearerAuthMiddleware(svc))

	entityCtrl := controllers.NewOrderController(svc.OrderService)
	orders := protected.Group("/orders")
	orders.Post("/",              entityCtrl.CreateOrder)
	orders.Get("/",               entityCtrl.ListOrders)
	orders.Get("/:order_number",   entityCtrl.GetByOrderNumber)
}

func main() {
	if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil {
		log.Println("warning: .env file not found, using environment")
	}

	db  := initializers.InitialDB()
	initializers.RunMigrations(db)
	svc := initializers.InitServices(db)

	app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{AppName: "ecommerce-api v1"})
	configureMiddleware(app)

	// Swagger endpoint protected by basic auth
	swaggerUser := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_USER")
	swaggerPass := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_PASSWORD")
	if swaggerUser == "" || swaggerPass == "" {
		log.Fatal("SWAGGER_USER and SWAGGER_PASSWORD must be set")
	}
	swaggerAuth := basicauth.New(basicauth.Config{
		Users: map[string]string{swaggerUser: swaggerPass},
	})
	app.Get("/swagger/*", swaggerAuth, swagger.HandlerDefault)

	registerRoutes(app, svc)

	port := os.Getenv("PORT")
	if port == "" {
		port = "8080"
	}
	log.Printf("server listening on :%s", port)
	if err := app.Listen(":" + port); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("server error: %v", err)
	}
}

// controllers/orderController.go
// Pattern: struct controller + constructor injection + Swagger annotations
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/controllers/
package controllers

import (
	"net/http"
	"time"

	"ecommerce-api/services/contracts"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// OrderController handles HTTP requests for the Order resource.
type OrderController struct {
	OrderService contracts.OrderServiceContract
}

// NewOrderController constructs a OrderController with its service dependency.
func NewOrderController(svc contracts.OrderServiceContract) *OrderController {
	return &OrderController{OrderService: svc}
}

// CreateOrder godoc
// @Summary     Create a new order
// @Description Create a new order in the system
// @Tags        Orders
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       body body contracts.OrderInputDTO true "Order data"
// @Success     201  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     400  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     422  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /orders [post]
func (ctrl *OrderController) CreateOrder(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var input contracts.OrderInputDTO
	if err := c.BodyParser(&input); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid request payload"})
	}

	if input.CustomerID == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "customer_id is required"})
	}
	if len(input.Items) == 0 {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "items cannot be empty"})
	}

	id, err := ctrl.OrderService.CreateFromInput(input)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusCreated).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"message": "Order created successfully",
		"id":      id,
	})
}

// GetByOrderNumber godoc
// @Summary     Get order by OrderNumber
// @Description Retrieve a single order identified by its OrderNumber
// @Tags        Orders
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       order_number path string true "Order OrderNumber"
// @Success     200 {object} contracts.OrderDTO
// @Failure     400 {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     404 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /orders/{order_number} [get]
func (ctrl *OrderController) GetByOrderNumber(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	identifier := c.Params("order_number")
	if identifier == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "order_number is required"})
	}

	result, err := ctrl.OrderService.FindByOrderNumber(identifier)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusNotFound).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(result)
}

// ListOrders godoc
// @Summary     List all orders
// @Description Returns a paginated list of orders
// @Tags        Orders
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       page  query int false "Page number (default 1)"
// @Param       limit query int false "Page size (default 20)"
// @Success     200  {array}  contracts.OrderDTO
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /orders [get]
func (ctrl *OrderController) ListOrders(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	page  := c.QueryInt("page", 1)
	limit := c.QueryInt("limit", 20)
	if page < 1  { page = 1 }
	if limit < 1 { limit = 20 }

	results, err := ctrl.OrderService.List(page, limit)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to retrieve orders"})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"data":  results,
		"page":  page,
		"limit": limit,
	})
}

// utils/responseUtil.go
// Pattern: response utility interface + JSON helper
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/utils/responseUtil.go
package utils

import (
	"encoding/json"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// ResponseUtil abstracts JSON response construction for testability.
type ResponseUtil interface {
	RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error
	RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error
}

// JSONResponseUtil implements ResponseUtil using Fiber's context.
type JSONResponseUtil struct{}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error {
	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).
			JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to marshal response"})
	}
	c.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	return c.Status(code).Send(body)
}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error {
	return c.Status(code).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": message})
}
</go_file>

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/basicauth"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/cors"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/logger"
	"github.com/gofiber/swagger"
	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
)

// bearerAuthMiddleware validates the Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
// Returns 401 if missing or invalid; calls Next() on success.
func bearerAuthMiddleware(svc *initializers.Services) fiber.Handler {
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		header := c.Get("Authorization")
		if len(header) <= 7 || header[:7] != "Bearer " {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "missing or malformed Authorization header",
			})
		}
		if err := svc.AuthTokenService.ValidateToken(header[7:]); err != nil {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error":  "invalid or expired token",
				"reason": err.Error(),
			})
		}
		return c.Next()
	}
}

func configureMiddleware(app *fiber.App) {
	app.Use(cors.New(cors.Config{
		AllowOrigins: "*",
		AllowMethods: "GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE,OPTIONS",
		AllowHeaders: "Origin, Content-Type, Accept, Authorization",
	}))
	app.Use(logger.New(logger.Config{
		Format: `[$\{time\}] $\{status\} - $\{method\} $\{path\}\n`,
	}))
}

func registerRoutes(app *fiber.App, svc *initializers.Services) {
	auth := app.Group("/auth")
	auth.Post("/token",    controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).GenerateToken)
	auth.Post("/validate", controllers.NewAuthController(svc.AuthTokenService).ValidateToken)

	protected := app.Group("/", bearerAuthMiddleware(svc))

	entityCtrl := controllers.NewEmployeeController(svc.EmployeeService)
	employees := protected.Group("/employees")
	employees.Post("/",              entityCtrl.CreateEmployee)
	employees.Get("/",               entityCtrl.ListEmployees)
	employees.Get("/:cpf",   entityCtrl.GetByCPF)
}

func main() {
	if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil {
		log.Println("warning: .env file not found, using environment")
	}

	db  := initializers.InitialDB()
	initializers.RunMigrations(db)
	svc := initializers.InitServices(db)

	app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{AppName: "hrm-api v1"})
	configureMiddleware(app)

	// Swagger endpoint protected by basic auth
	swaggerUser := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_USER")
	swaggerPass := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_PASSWORD")
	if swaggerUser == "" || swaggerPass == "" {
		log.Fatal("SWAGGER_USER and SWAGGER_PASSWORD must be set")
	}
	swaggerAuth := basicauth.New(basicauth.Config{
		Users: map[string]string{swaggerUser: swaggerPass},
	})
	app.Get("/swagger/*", swaggerAuth, swagger.HandlerDefault)

	registerRoutes(app, svc)

	port := os.Getenv("PORT")
	if port == "" {
		port = "8080"
	}
	log.Printf("server listening on :%s", port)
	if err := app.Listen(":" + port); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("server error: %v", err)
	}
}

// controllers/employeeController.go
// Pattern: struct controller + constructor injection + Swagger annotations
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/controllers/
package controllers

import (
	"net/http"
	"time"

	"hrm-api/services/contracts"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// EmployeeController handles HTTP requests for the Employee resource.
type EmployeeController struct {
	EmployeeService contracts.EmployeeServiceContract
}

// NewEmployeeController constructs a EmployeeController with its service dependency.
func NewEmployeeController(svc contracts.EmployeeServiceContract) *EmployeeController {
	return &EmployeeController{EmployeeService: svc}
}

// CreateEmployee godoc
// @Summary     Create a new employee
// @Description Create a new employee in the system
// @Tags        Employees
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       body body contracts.EmployeeInputDTO true "Employee data"
// @Success     201  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     400  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     422  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /employees [post]
func (ctrl *EmployeeController) CreateEmployee(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var input contracts.EmployeeInputDTO
	if err := c.BodyParser(&input); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid request payload"})
	}

	if input.DepartmentName == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "department_name is required"})
	}
	if input.Position == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "position is required"})
	}

	id, err := ctrl.EmployeeService.CreateFromInput(input)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusCreated).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"message": "Employee created successfully",
		"id":      id,
	})
}

// GetByCPF godoc
// @Summary     Get employee by CPF
// @Description Retrieve a single employee identified by its CPF
// @Tags        Employees
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       cpf path string true "Employee CPF"
// @Success     200 {object} contracts.EmployeeDTO
// @Failure     400 {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     404 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /employees/{cpf} [get]
func (ctrl *EmployeeController) GetByCPF(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	identifier := c.Params("cpf")
	if identifier == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "cpf is required"})
	}

	result, err := ctrl.EmployeeService.FindByCPF(identifier)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusNotFound).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(result)
}

// ListEmployees godoc
// @Summary     List all employees
// @Description Returns a paginated list of employees
// @Tags        Employees
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       page  query int false "Page number (default 1)"
// @Param       limit query int false "Page size (default 20)"
// @Success     200  {array}  contracts.EmployeeDTO
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /employees [get]
func (ctrl *EmployeeController) ListEmployees(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	page  := c.QueryInt("page", 1)
	limit := c.QueryInt("limit", 20)
	if page < 1  { page = 1 }
	if limit < 1 { limit = 20 }

	results, err := ctrl.EmployeeService.List(page, limit)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to retrieve employees"})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"data":  results,
		"page":  page,
		"limit": limit,
	})
}

// utils/responseUtil.go
// Pattern: response utility interface + JSON helper
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/utils/responseUtil.go
package utils

import (
	"encoding/json"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// ResponseUtil abstracts JSON response construction for testability.
type ResponseUtil interface {
	RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error
	RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error
}

// JSONResponseUtil implements ResponseUtil using Fiber's context.
type JSONResponseUtil struct{}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error {
	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).
			JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to marshal response"})
	}
	c.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	return c.Status(code).Send(body)
}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error {
	return c.Status(code).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": message})
}

// @Failure     422  {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /shipments [post]
func (ctrl *ShipmentController) CreateShipment(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var input contracts.ShipmentInputDTO
	if err := c.BodyParser(&input); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid request payload"})
	}

	if input.OriginAddress == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "origin_address is required"})
	}
	if input.DestinationAddress == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "destination_address is required"})
	}

	id, err := ctrl.ShipmentService.CreateFromInput(input)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusCreated).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"message": "Shipment created successfully",
		"id":      id,
	})
}

// GetByTrackingCode godoc
// @Summary     Get shipment by TrackingCode
// @Description Retrieve a single shipment identified by its TrackingCode
// @Tags        Shipments
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       tracking_code path string true "Shipment TrackingCode"
// @Success     200 {object} contracts.ShipmentDTO
// @Failure     400 {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     404 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /shipments/{tracking_code} [get]
func (ctrl *ShipmentController) GetByTrackingCode(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	identifier := c.Params("tracking_code")
	if identifier == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "tracking_code is required"})
	}

	result, err := ctrl.ShipmentService.FindByTrackingCode(identifier)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusNotFound).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": err.Error()})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(result)
}

// ListShipments godoc
// @Summary     List all shipments
// @Description Returns a paginated list of shipments
// @Tags        Shipments
// @Produce     json
// @Security    BearerAuth
// @Param       page  query int false "Page number (default 1)"
// @Param       limit query int false "Page size (default 20)"
// @Success     200  {array}  contracts.ShipmentDTO
// @Failure     500  {object} fiber.Map

func (ctrl *ShipmentController) ListShipments(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	page  := c.QueryInt("page", 1)
	limit := c.QueryInt("limit", 20)
	if page < 1  { page = 1 }
	if limit < 1 { limit = 20 }

	results, err := ctrl.ShipmentService.List(page, limit)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to retrieve shipments"})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"data":  results,
		"page":  page,
		"limit": limit,
	})
}

	shipments.Get("/:tracking_code",   entityCtrl.GetByTrackingCode)
}

func main() {
	if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil {
		log.Println("warning: .env file not found, using environment")
	}

	db  := initializers.InitialDB()
	initializers.RunMigrations(db)
	svc := initializers.InitServices(db)

	app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{AppName: "logistics-api v1"})
	configureMiddleware(app)

	// Swagger endpoint protected by basic auth
	swaggerUser := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_USER")
	swaggerPass := os.Getenv("SWAGGER_PASSWORD")
	if swaggerUser == "" || swaggerPass == "" {
		log.Fatal("SWAGGER_USER and SWAGGER_PASSWORD must be set")
	}
	swaggerAuth := basicauth.New(basicauth.Config{
		Users: map[string]string{swaggerUser: swaggerPass},
	})
	app.Get("/swagger/*", swaggerAuth, swagger.HandlerDefault)

	registerRoutes(app, svc)

	port := os.Getenv("PORT")
	if port == "" {
		port = "8080"
	}
	log.Printf("server listening on :%s", port)
	if err := app.Listen(":" + port); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("server error: %v", err)
	}
}

// utils/responseUtil.go
// Pattern: response utility interface + JSON helper
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/utils/responseUtil.go
package utils

import (
	"encoding/json"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// ResponseUtil abstracts JSON response construction for testability.
type ResponseUtil interface {
	RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error
	RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error
}

// JSONResponseUtil implements ResponseUtil using Fiber's context.
type JSONResponseUtil struct{}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithJSON(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, payload any) error {
	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).
			JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to marshal response"})
	}
	c.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	return c.Status(code).Send(body)
}

func (ru *JSONResponseUtil) RespondWithError(c *fiber.Ctx, code int, message string) error {
	return c.Status(code).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": message})
}

package repositories

import (

	"medical-sas-api/domain/dtos"
	"medical-sas-api/domain/entities"
	"github.com/google/uuid"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// PatientRepositoryImpl implements contracts.PatientRepository using GORM.
type PatientRepositoryImpl struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// NewPatientRepository constructs a new PatientRepositoryImpl.
func NewPatientRepository(db *gorm.DB) *PatientRepositoryImpl {
	return &PatientRepositoryImpl{db: db}
}

func (r *PatientRepositoryImpl) Create(dto dtos.PatientDTO) (uuid.UUID, error) {
	entity := entities.Patient{} // map DTO → entity fields
	// (field mapping omitted for brevity — use a mapper or manual assignment)
	if err := r.db.Create(&entity).Error; err != nil {
		return uuid.Nil, fmt.Errorf("patient create: %w", err)
	}
	return entity.ID, nil
}

func (r *PatientRepositoryImpl) FindByID(id uuid.UUID) (*dtos.PatientDTO, error) {
	var entity entities.Patient
	if err := r.db.First(&entity, "id = ?", id).Error; err != nil {
		if err == gorm.ErrRecordNotFound {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("patient not found: %s", id)
		}
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("patient find: %w", err)
	}
	dto := dtos.PatientDTO{} // map entity → DTO
	return &dto, nil
}

func (r *PatientRepositoryImpl) FindByCPF(cpf string) (*dtos.PatientDTO, error) {
	var entity entities.Patient
	if err := r.db.Where("cpf = ?", cpf).First(&entity).Error; err != nil {
		if err == gorm.ErrRecordNotFound {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("patient with cpf=%s not found", cpf)
		}
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("patient find by cpf: %w", err)
	}
	dto := dtos.PatientDTO{}
	return &dto, nil
}

func (r *PatientRepositoryImpl) List(page, limit int) ([]dtos.PatientDTO, error) {
	var entities []entities.Patient
	offset := (page - 1) * limit
	if err := r.db.Offset(offset).Limit(limit).Find(&entities).Error; err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("patient list: %w", err)
	}
	dtos := make([]dtos.PatientDTO, 0, len(entities))
	// map each entity to DTO
	return dtos, nil
}

func (r *PatientRepositoryImpl) Update(id uuid.UUID, dto dtos.PatientDTO) error {
	result := r.db.Model(&entities.Patient{}).Where("id = ?", id).Updates(dto)
	if result.Error != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("patient update: %w", result.Error)
	}
	if result.RowsAffected == 0 {
		return fmt.Errorf("patient not found: %s", id)
	}
	return nil
}

func (r *PatientRepositoryImpl) Delete(id uuid.UUID) error {
	// GORM soft delete: sets deleted_at instead of removing the row
	result := r.db.Delete(&entities.Patient{}, "id = ?", id)
	if result.Error != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("patient delete: %w", result.Error)
	}
	if result.RowsAffected == 0 {
		return fmt.Errorf("patient not found: %s", id)
	}
	return nil
}

// initializers/database.go
// Pattern: GORM + PostgreSQL connection with pool config + env-based DSN
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/initializers/database.go
package initializers

import (
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"gorm.io/gorm/logger"
)

// InitialDB opens a PostgreSQL connection using environment variables.
// Panics on failure — the app cannot run without a database.
func InitialDB() *gorm.DB {
	dsn := buildDSN()

	db, err := gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{
		Logger: logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Info),
	})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("database connection failed: %v", err)
	}

	sqlDB, err := db.DB()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("failed to get underlying sql.DB: %v", err)
	}
	sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(25)
	sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(10)
	sqlDB.SetConnMaxLifetime(5 * time.Minute)

	log.Println("database connected:", maskDSN(dsn))
	return db
}

func buildDSN() string {
	return fmt.Sprintf(
		"host=%s port=%s user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=%s TimeZone=%s",
		getEnv("DB_HOST",     "localhost"),
		getEnv("DB_PORT",     "5432"),
		getEnv("DB_USER",     "postgres"),
		os.Getenv("DB_PASSWORD"),
		getEnv("DB_NAME",     "appdb"),
		getEnv("DB_SSLMODE",  "disable"),
		getEnv("DB_TIMEZONE", "UTC"),
	)
}

func getEnv(key, fallback string) string {
	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
		return v
	}
	return fallback
}

func maskDSN(dsn string) string {
	password := os.Getenv("DB_PASSWORD")
	if password == "" {
		return dsn
	}
	return strings.ReplaceAll(dsn, password, "*****")
}

// services/contracts/patientServiceContract.go
// Pattern: service interface decouples controller from implementation
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/services/contracts/
package contracts

import "github.com/google/uuid"

// PatientServiceContract is the interface every Patient service implementation must satisfy.
type PatientServiceContract interface {
	// CreateFromInput resolves human-readable names in the InputDTO to UUIDs,
	// then delegates persistence to the repository.
	CreateFromInput(input PatientInputDTO) (uuid.UUID, error)

	FindByCPF(cpf string) (*PatientDTO, error)

	List(page, limit int) ([]PatientDTO, error)

	Update(id uuid.UUID, dto PatientDTO) error

	Delete(id uuid.UUID) error
}

// PatientInputDTO carries fields exactly as received from the HTTP layer.
// Name-based fields (OrganizationName, etc.) are resolved in the service.
type PatientInputDTO struct {
	OrganizationName string `json:"organization_name"`
	Name    string `json:"name"`
	Contact string `json:"contact"`
	CPF     string `json:"cpf,omitempty"`
}

// PatientDTO is the canonical data transfer object passed between layers.
type PatientDTO struct {
	OrganizationID string `json:"organization_id"`
	Name    string `json:"name"`
	Contact string `json:"contact"`
	CPF     string `json:"cpf,omitempty"`
}
</go_file>

// services/implementations/patientService.go
// Pattern: service implementation resolves names→IDs, validates, then calls repo
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/services/implementations/
package implementations

import (
	"fmt"

	"medical-sas-api/domain/repositories/contracts"
	sc "$module/services/contracts"
	"github.com/google/uuid"
	"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
)

// PatientService implements sc.PatientServiceContract.
type PatientService struct {
	repo         contracts.PatientRepository
	orgRepo      contracts.OrganizationRepository
}

// NewPatientService constructs a PatientService with all required repositories.
func NewPatientService(
	repo    contracts.PatientRepository,
	orgRepo contracts.OrganizationRepository,
) *PatientService {
	return &PatientService{repo: repo, orgRepo: orgRepo}
}

// CreateFromInput resolves organization name to UUID in parallel with any other
// lookups, validates the result, then inserts via the repository.
func (s *PatientService) CreateFromInput(input sc.PatientInputDTO) (uuid.UUID, error) {
	var (
		orgID uuid.UUID
	)

	// Parallel resolution of related entities
	g := &errgroup.Group{}
	g.Go(func() error {
		org, err := s.orgRepo.FindByName(input.OrganizationName)
		if err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("organization %q not found: %w", input.OrganizationName, err)
		}
		orgID = org.ID
		return nil
	})
	if err := g.Wait(); err != nil {
		return uuid.Nil, err
	}

	dto := sc.PatientDTO{
		OrganizationID: orgID.String(),
		// map remaining input fields
	}
	return s.repo.Create(dto)
}

func (s *PatientService) FindByCPF(cpf string) (*sc.PatientDTO, error) {
	result, err := s.repo.FindByCPF(cpf)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("patient find by cpf: %w", err)
	}
	return result, nil
}

func (s *PatientService) List(page, limit int) ([]sc.PatientDTO, error) {
	return s.repo.List(page, limit)
}

func (s *PatientService) Update(id uuid.UUID, dto sc.PatientDTO) error {
	return s.repo.Update(id, dto)
}

func (s *PatientService) Delete(id uuid.UUID) error {
	return s.repo.Delete(id)
}
</go_file>

	List(page, limit int) ([]AppointmentDTO, error)

	Update(id uuid.UUID, dto AppointmentDTO) error

	Delete(id uuid.UUID) error
}

// AppointmentInputDTO carries fields exactly as received from the HTTP layer.
// Name-based fields (OrganizationName, etc.) are resolved in the service.
type AppointmentInputDTO struct {
	OrganizationName string `json:"organization_name"`

	DoctorFullName string    `json:"doctor_full_name"`

}

// AppointmentDTO is the canonical data transfer object passed between layers.
type AppointmentDTO struct {
	OrganizationID string `json:"organization_id"`

	DoctorID       string    `json:"doctor_id"`

}

// services/implementations/appointmentService.go
// Pattern: service implementation resolves names→IDs, validates, then calls repo
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/services/implementations/
package implementations

import (
	"fmt"

	"medical-sas-api/domain/repositories/contracts"
	sc "$module/services/contracts"
	"github.com/google/uuid"
	"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
)

// AppointmentService implements sc.AppointmentServiceContract.
type AppointmentService struct {
	repo         contracts.AppointmentRepository
	orgRepo      contracts.OrganizationRepository
}

// NewAppointmentService constructs a AppointmentService with all required repositories.
func NewAppointmentService(
	repo    contracts.AppointmentRepository,
	orgRepo contracts.OrganizationRepository,
) *AppointmentService {
	return &AppointmentService{repo: repo, orgRepo: orgRepo}
}

// CreateFromInput resolves organization name to UUID in parallel with any other
// lookups, validates the result, then inserts via the repository.
func (s *AppointmentService) CreateFromInput(input sc.AppointmentInputDTO) (uuid.UUID, error) {
	var (
		orgID uuid.UUID
	)

	// Parallel resolution of related entities
	g := &errgroup.Group{}
	g.Go(func() error {
		org, err := s.orgRepo.FindByName(input.OrganizationName)
		if err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("organization %q not found: %w", input.OrganizationName, err)
		}
		orgID = org.ID
		return nil
	})
	if err := g.Wait(); err != nil {
		return uuid.Nil, err
	}

	dto := sc.AppointmentDTO{
		OrganizationID: orgID.String(),
		// map remaining input fields
	}
	return s.repo.Create(dto)
}

func (s *AppointmentService) FindByStatus(status string) (*sc.AppointmentDTO, error) {
	result, err := s.repo.FindByStatus(status)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("appointment find by status: %w", err)
	}
	return result, nil
}

func (s *AppointmentService) List(page, limit int) ([]sc.AppointmentDTO, error) {
	return s.repo.List(page, limit)
}

func (s *AppointmentService) Update(id uuid.UUID, dto sc.AppointmentDTO) error {
	return s.repo.Update(id, dto)
}

func (s *AppointmentService) Delete(id uuid.UUID) error {
	return s.repo.Delete(id)
}
</go_file>

// services/events/doctors/doctorEventConsumer.go
// Pattern: RabbitMQ consumer with reconnect loop + JSON unmarshaling
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/services/events/
package events

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/streadway/amqp"
)

// DoctorCreatedEvent is the message payload published when a doctor is registered.
type DoctorCreatedEvent struct {
	DoctorID   string    `json:"doctor_id"`
	FullName   string    `json:"full_name"`
	Specialty  string    `json:"specialty"`
	CreatedAt  time.Time `json:"created_at"`
}

// DoctorEventConsumer subscribes to the doctors.created queue.
type DoctorEventConsumer struct {
	conn    *amqp.Connection
	ch      *amqp.Channel
	queue   string
	handler func(DoctorCreatedEvent) error
}

// NewDoctorEventConsumer dials RabbitMQ and declares the queue.
func NewDoctorEventConsumer(handler func(DoctorCreatedEvent) error) (*DoctorEventConsumer, error) {
	url := os.Getenv("RABBITMQ_BASE_URL")
	if url == "" {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("RABBITMQ_BASE_URL is not set")
	}

	conn, err := amqp.Dial(url)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("rabbitmq dial: %w", err)
	}

	ch, err := conn.Channel()
	if err != nil {
		conn.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("rabbitmq channel: %w", err)
	}

	queue := "doctors.created"
	_, err = ch.QueueDeclare(queue, true, false, false, false, nil)
	if err != nil {
		ch.Close(); conn.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("queue declare: %w", err)
	}

	return &DoctorEventConsumer{conn: conn, ch: ch, queue: queue, handler: handler}, nil
}

// Consume starts consuming messages and blocks until ctx is cancelled.
func (c *DoctorEventConsumer) Consume() error {
	msgs, err := c.ch.Consume(c.queue, "", false, false, false, false, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("consume: %w", err)
	}

	log.Printf("consuming from %s", c.queue)
	for msg := range msgs {
		var event DoctorCreatedEvent
		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg.Body, &event); err != nil {
			log.Printf("unmarshal error: %v — nacking", err)
			msg.Nack(false, false) // dead-letter
			continue
		}
		if err := c.handler(event); err != nil {
			log.Printf("handler error: %v — nacking with requeue", err)
			msg.Nack(false, true)
			continue
		}
		msg.Ack(false)
	}
	return nil
}

// Close releases RabbitMQ resources.
func (c *DoctorEventConsumer) Close() {
	c.ch.Close()
	c.conn.Close()
}
</go_file>

// services/implementations/auth_service.go
// Pattern: JWT HS256 token generation + validation service
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/services/implementations/auth_service.go
package implementations

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v4"
	"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
)

// Claims holds the JWT payload.
type Claims struct {
	UserID string `json:"user_id"`
	Email  string `json:"email"`
	Role   string `json:"role"`
	jwt.RegisteredClaims
}

// AuthTokenServiceImpl handles JWT creation and validation.
type AuthTokenServiceImpl struct {
	secret []byte
	ttl    time.Duration
}

// NewAuthTokenService reads JWT_SECRET from the environment and constructs the service.
func NewAuthTokenService() *AuthTokenServiceImpl {
	secret := os.Getenv("JWT_SECRET")
	if secret == "" {
		panic("JWT_SECRET environment variable must be set")
	}
	return &AuthTokenServiceImpl{
		secret: []byte(secret),
		ttl:    24 * time.Hour,
	}
}

// GenerateToken creates a signed HS256 JWT for the given user attributes.
func (s *AuthTokenServiceImpl) GenerateToken(userID, email, role string) (string, error) {
	claims := &Claims{
		UserID: userID,
		Email:  email,
		Role:   role,
		RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
			ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(s.ttl)),
			IssuedAt:  jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
			Issuer:    "medical-sas-api",
		},
	}
	token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
	signed, err := token.SignedString(s.secret)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("sign token: %w", err)
	}
	return signed, nil
}

// ValidateToken parses and validates a JWT string. Returns nil on success.
func (s *AuthTokenServiceImpl) ValidateToken(tokenString string) error {
	token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, &Claims{}, func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
		if _, ok := t.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected signing method: %v", t.Header["alg"])
		}
		return s.secret, nil
	})
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid token: %w", err)
	}
	if !token.Valid {
		return fmt.Errorf("token is not valid")
	}
	return nil
}

// ParseClaims extracts the Claims from a valid JWT string.
func (s *AuthTokenServiceImpl) ParseClaims(tokenString string) (*Claims, error) {
	claims := &Claims{}
	_, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, claims, func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
		return s.secret, nil
	})
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse claims: %w", err)
	}
	return claims, nil
}

// HashPassword returns a bcrypt hash of the plain-text password.
func HashPassword(plain string) (string, error) {
	hashed, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(plain), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("hash password: %w", err)
	}
	return string(hashed), nil
}

// CheckPassword returns nil if plain matches the bcrypt hash.
func CheckPassword(plain, hash string) error {
	if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(hash), []byte(plain)); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid credentials")
	}
	return nil
}

// middleware/auth.go
// Pattern: Fiber JWT bearer middleware with claims propagation via Locals
// Observed pattern from: Medical-App-Core/cmd/main.go AuthMiddleware
package middleware

import (
	"strings"

	"medical-sas-api/services/implementations"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// BearerAuth validates the Authorization: Bearer header and stores parsed
// claims in fiber.Ctx.Locals("claims") for downstream handlers.
func BearerAuth(authSvc *implementations.AuthTokenServiceImpl) fiber.Handler {
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		header := c.Get("Authorization")
		if !strings.HasPrefix(header, "Bearer ") {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "missing or malformed Authorization header",
			})
		}

		tokenStr := strings.TrimPrefix(header, "Bearer ")
		claims, err := authSvc.ParseClaims(tokenStr)
		if err != nil {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error":  "invalid or expired token",
				"reason": err.Error(),
			})
		}

		// Make claims available to all downstream handlers
		c.Locals("claims", claims)
		c.Locals("user_id", claims.UserID)
		c.Locals("role", claims.Role)
		return c.Next()
	}
}

// RoleRequired returns a middleware that allows only the specified roles.
// Must be chained after BearerAuth.
func RoleRequired(roles ...string) fiber.Handler {
	allowed := make(map[string]struct{}, len(roles))
	for _, r := range roles {
		allowed[r] = struct{}{}
	}
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		role, _ := c.Locals("role").(string)
		if _, ok := allowed[role]; !ok {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusForbidden).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "insufficient permissions",
			})
		}
		return c.Next()
	}
}

// controllers/auth_controller.go
// Pattern: login + token generation handler
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/controllers/auth_controller.go
package controllers

import (
	"net/http"

	"medical-sas-api/services/implementations"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// AuthController handles token generation and validation endpoints.
type AuthController struct {
	authSvc *implementations.AuthTokenServiceImpl
}

func NewAuthController(svc *implementations.AuthTokenServiceImpl) *AuthController {
	return &AuthController{authSvc: svc}
}

type generateTokenRequest struct {
	UserID string `json:"user_id" validate:"required,uuid4"`
	Email  string `json:"email"   validate:"required,email"`
	Role   string `json:"role"    validate:"required"`
}

// GenerateToken godoc
// @Summary     Issue a JWT token
// @Description Generates a signed JWT for the given user credentials
// @Tags        Auth
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Param       body body generateTokenRequest true "Token request"
// @Success     200 {object} fiber.Map{token=string}
// @Failure     400 {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     500 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /auth/token [post]
func (ac *AuthController) GenerateToken(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var req generateTokenRequest
	if err := c.BodyParser(&req); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid request payload"})
	}
	if req.UserID == "" || req.Email == "" || req.Role == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "user_id, email, and role are required"})
	}

	token, err := ac.authSvc.GenerateToken(req.UserID, req.Email, req.Role)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to generate token"})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{"token": token})
}

// ValidateToken godoc
// @Summary     Validate a JWT token
// @Description Returns 200 if the token is valid, 401 otherwise
// @Tags        Auth
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Param       body body fiber.Map{token=string} true "Token to validate"
// @Success     200 {object} fiber.Map{valid=bool}
// @Failure     401 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /auth/validate [post]
func (ac *AuthController) ValidateToken(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var body struct {
		Token string `json:"token"`
	}
	if err := c.BodyParser(&body); err != nil || body.Token == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "token is required"})
	}

	if err := ac.authSvc.ValidateToken(body.Token); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
			"valid":  false,
			"reason": err.Error(),
		})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{"valid": true})
}

// initializers/validators.go
// Pattern: custom validator registration with go-playground/validator
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/initializers/validators.go
package initializers

import (
	"strconv"
	"strings"

	"github.com/go-playground/validator/v10"
)

// RegisterCustomValidators adds domain-specific validation rules.
func RegisterCustomValidators(v *validator.Validate) {
	_ = v.RegisterValidation("cpf",  validateCPF)
	_ = v.RegisterValidation("cnpj", validateCNPJ)
	_ = v.RegisterValidation("crm",  validateCRM)
}

// validateCPF validates a Brazilian CPF number (11 digits, checksum).
func validateCPF(fl validator.FieldLevel) bool {
	cpf := strings.ReplaceAll(fl.Field().String(), ".", "")
	cpf  = strings.ReplaceAll(cpf, "-", "")
	if len(cpf) != 11 || allEqual(cpf) {
		return false
	}
	return cpfChecksum(cpf)
}

func cpfChecksum(cpf string) bool {
	digits := make([]int, 11)
	for i, ch := range cpf {
		digits[i], _ = strconv.Atoi(string(ch))
	}
	for pass := 0; pass < 2; pass++ {
		weight := 10 + pass
		sum    := 0
		for i := 0; i < 9+pass; i++ {
			sum += digits[i] * (weight - i)
		}
		rem := (sum * 10) % 11
		if rem == 10 || rem == 11 {
			rem = 0
		}
		if rem != digits[9+pass] {
			return false
		}
	}
	return true
}

// validateCNPJ validates a Brazilian CNPJ number (14 digits, checksum).
func validateCNPJ(fl validator.FieldLevel) bool {
	cnpj := strings.Map(func(r rune) rune {
		if r >= '0' && r <= '9' { return r }
		return -1
	}, fl.Field().String())
	if len(cnpj) != 14 || allEqual(cnpj) {
		return false
	}
	return cnpjChecksum(cnpj)
}

func cnpjChecksum(cnpj string) bool {
	weights1 := []int{5, 4, 3, 2, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2}
	weights2 := []int{6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2}
	for pass, weights := range [][]int{weights1, weights2} {
		sum := 0
		for i, w := range weights {
			d, _ := strconv.Atoi(string(cnpj[i]))
			sum += d * w
		}
		rem := sum % 11
		expected := byte('0')
		if rem >= 2 {
			expected = byte('0' + (11 - rem))
		}
		if cnpj[12+pass] != expected {
			return false
		}
	}
	return true
}

// validateCRM validates a Brazilian CRM number (digits optionally followed by UF code).
func validateCRM(fl validator.FieldLevel) bool {
	crm := fl.Field().String()
	return len(crm) >= 4 && len(crm) <= 10
}

func allEqual(s string) bool {
	if s == "" { return false }
	for _, c := range s[1:] {
		if byte(c) != s[0] { return false }
	}
	return true
}

	return nil

		return s.secret, nil
	})

	}

// ParseClaims extracts the Claims from a valid JWT string.
func (s *AuthTokenServiceImpl) ParseClaims(tokenString string) (*Claims, error) {
	claims := &Claims{}
	_, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, claims, func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
		return s.secret, nil
	})
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse claims: %w", err)
	}
	return claims, nil
}

// HashPassword returns a bcrypt hash of the plain-text password.
func HashPassword(plain string) (string, error) {
	hashed, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(plain), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("hash password: %w", err)
	}
	return string(hashed), nil
}

// CheckPassword returns nil if plain matches the bcrypt hash.
func CheckPassword(plain, hash string) error {
	if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(hash), []byte(plain)); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid credentials")
	}
	return nil
}

// middleware/auth.go
// Pattern: Fiber JWT bearer middleware with claims propagation via Locals
// Observed pattern from: Medical-App-Core/cmd/main.go AuthMiddleware
package middleware

import (
	"strings"

	"medical-sas-api/services/implementations"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// BearerAuth validates the Authorization: Bearer header and stores parsed
// claims in fiber.Ctx.Locals("claims") for downstream handlers.
func BearerAuth(authSvc *implementations.AuthTokenServiceImpl) fiber.Handler {
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		header := c.Get("Authorization")
		if !strings.HasPrefix(header, "Bearer ") {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "missing or malformed Authorization header",
			})
		}

		tokenStr := strings.TrimPrefix(header, "Bearer ")
		claims, err := authSvc.ParseClaims(tokenStr)
		if err != nil {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error":  "invalid or expired token",
				"reason": err.Error(),
			})
		}

		// Make claims available to all downstream handlers
		c.Locals("claims", claims)
		c.Locals("user_id", claims.UserID)
		c.Locals("role", claims.Role)
		return c.Next()
	}
}

// RoleRequired returns a middleware that allows only the specified roles.
// Must be chained after BearerAuth.
func RoleRequired(roles ...string) fiber.Handler {
	allowed := make(map[string]struct{}, len(roles))
	for _, r := range roles {
		allowed[r] = struct{}{}
	}
	return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		role, _ := c.Locals("role").(string)
		if _, ok := allowed[role]; !ok {
			return c.Status(fiber.StatusForbidden).JSON(fiber.Map{
				"error": "insufficient permissions",
			})
		}
		return c.Next()
	}
}

// controllers/auth_controller.go
// Pattern: login + token generation handler
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/controllers/auth_controller.go
package controllers

import (
	"net/http"

	"medical-sas-api/services/implementations"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// AuthController handles token generation and validation endpoints.
type AuthController struct {
	authSvc *implementations.AuthTokenServiceImpl
}

func NewAuthController(svc *implementations.AuthTokenServiceImpl) *AuthController {
	return &AuthController{authSvc: svc}
}

type generateTokenRequest struct {
	UserID string `json:"user_id" validate:"required,uuid4"`
	Email  string `json:"email"   validate:"required,email"`
	Role   string `json:"role"    validate:"required"`
}

// GenerateToken godoc
// @Summary     Issue a JWT token
// @Description Generates a signed JWT for the given user credentials
// @Tags        Auth
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Param       body body generateTokenRequest true "Token request"
// @Success     200 {object} fiber.Map{token=string}
// @Failure     400 {object} fiber.Map
// @Failure     500 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /auth/token [post]
func (ac *AuthController) GenerateToken(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var req generateTokenRequest
	if err := c.BodyParser(&req); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid request payload"})
	}
	if req.UserID == "" || req.Email == "" || req.Role == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "user_id, email, and role are required"})
	}

	token, err := ac.authSvc.GenerateToken(req.UserID, req.Email, req.Role)
	if err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "failed to generate token"})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{"token": token})
}

// ValidateToken godoc
// @Summary     Validate a JWT token
// @Description Returns 200 if the token is valid, 401 otherwise
// @Tags        Auth
// @Accept      json
// @Produce     json
// @Param       body body fiber.Map{token=string} true "Token to validate"
// @Success     200 {object} fiber.Map{valid=bool}
// @Failure     401 {object} fiber.Map
// @Router      /auth/validate [post]
func (ac *AuthController) ValidateToken(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var body struct {
		Token string `json:"token"`
	}
	if err := c.BodyParser(&body); err != nil || body.Token == "" {
		return c.Status(http.StatusBadRequest).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "token is required"})
	}

	if err := ac.authSvc.ValidateToken(body.Token); err != nil {
		return c.Status(http.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{
			"valid":  false,
			"reason": err.Error(),
		})
	}

	return c.Status(http.StatusOK).JSON(fiber.Map{"valid": true})
}

// initializers/validators.go
// Pattern: custom validator registration with go-playground/validator
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/initializers/validators.go
package initializers

import (
	"strconv"
	"strings"

	"github.com/go-playground/validator/v10"
)

// RegisterCustomValidators adds domain-specific validation rules.
func RegisterCustomValidators(v *validator.Validate) {
	_ = v.RegisterValidation("cpf",  validateCPF)
	_ = v.RegisterValidation("cnpj", validateCNPJ)
	_ = v.RegisterValidation("crm",  validateCRM)
}

// validateCPF validates a Brazilian CPF number (11 digits, checksum).
func validateCPF(fl validator.FieldLevel) bool {
	cpf := strings.ReplaceAll(fl.Field().String(), ".", "")
	cpf  = strings.ReplaceAll(cpf, "-", "")
	if len(cpf) != 11 || allEqual(cpf) {
		return false
	}
	return cpfChecksum(cpf)
}

func cpfChecksum(cpf string) bool {
	digits := make([]int, 11)
	for i, ch := range cpf {
		digits[i], _ = strconv.Atoi(string(ch))
	}
	for pass := 0; pass < 2; pass++ {
		weight := 10 + pass
		sum    := 0
		for i := 0; i < 9+pass; i++ {
			sum += digits[i] * (weight - i)
		}
		rem := (sum * 10) % 11
		if rem == 10 || rem == 11 {
			rem = 0
		}
		if rem != digits[9+pass] {
			return false
		}
	}
	return true
}

// validateCNPJ validates a Brazilian CNPJ number (14 digits, checksum).
func validateCNPJ(fl validator.FieldLevel) bool {
	cnpj := strings.Map(func(r rune) rune {
		if r >= '0' && r <= '9' { return r }
		return -1
	}, fl.Field().String())
	if len(cnpj) != 14 || allEqual(cnpj) {
		return false
	}
	return cnpjChecksum(cnpj)
}

func cnpjChecksum(cnpj string) bool {
	weights1 := []int{5, 4, 3, 2, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2}
	weights2 := []int{6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2}
	for pass, weights := range [][]int{weights1, weights2} {
		sum := 0
		for i, w := range weights {
			d, _ := strconv.Atoi(string(cnpj[i]))
			sum += d * w
		}
		rem := sum % 11
		expected := byte('0')
		if rem >= 2 {
			expected = byte('0' + (11 - rem))
		}
		if cnpj[12+pass] != expected {
			return false
		}
	}
	return true
}

// validateCRM validates a Brazilian CRM number (digits optionally followed by UF code).
func validateCRM(fl validator.FieldLevel) bool {
	crm := fl.Field().String()
	return len(crm) >= 4 && len(crm) <= 10
}

func allEqual(s string) bool {
	if s == "" { return false }
	for _, c := range s[1:] {
		if byte(c) != s[0] { return false }
	}
	return true
}
</go_file>

// services/implementations/auth_service.go
// Pattern: JWT HS256 token generation + validation service
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/services/implementations/auth_service.go
package implementations

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v4"
	"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
)

// Claims holds the JWT payload.
type Claims struct {
	UserID string `json:"user_id"`
	Email  string `json:"email"`
	Role   string `json:"role"`
	jwt.RegisteredClaims
}

// AuthTokenServiceImpl handles JWT creation and validation.
type AuthTokenServiceImpl struct {
	secret []byte
	ttl    time.Duration
}

// NewAuthTokenService reads JWT_SECRET from the environment and constructs the service.
func NewAuthTokenService() *AuthTokenServiceImpl {
	secret := os.Getenv("JWT_SECRET")
	if secret == "" {
		panic("JWT_SECRET environment variable must be set")
	}
	return &AuthTokenServiceImpl{
		secret: []byte(secret),
		ttl:    24 * time.Hour,
	}
}

// GenerateToken creates a signed HS256 JWT for the given user attributes.
func (s *AuthTokenServiceImpl) GenerateToken(userID, email, role string) (string, error) {
	claims := &Claims{
		UserID: userID,
		Email:  email,
		Role:   role,
		RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
			ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(s.ttl)),
			IssuedAt:  jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
			Issuer:    "medical-sas-api",
		},
	}
	token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
	signed, err := token.SignedString(s.secret)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("sign token: %w", err)
	}
	return signed, nil
}

// ValidateToken parses and validates a JWT string. Returns nil on success.
func (s *AuthTokenServiceImpl) ValidateToken(tokenString string) error {
	token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, &Claims{}, func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
		if _, ok := t.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected signing method: %v", t.Header["alg"])
		}
		return s.secret, nil
	})
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid token: %w", err)
	}
	if !token.Valid {
		return fmt.Errorf("token is not valid")
	}
	return nil
}

// ParseClaims extracts the Claims from a valid JWT string.
func (s *AuthTokenServiceImpl) ParseClaims(tokenString string) (*Claims, error) {
	claims := &Claims{}
	_, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, claims, func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
		return s.secret, nil
	})
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse claims: %w", err)
	}
	return claims, nil
}

// HashPassword returns a bcrypt hash of the plain-text password.
func HashPassword(plain string) (string, error) {
	hashed, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(plain), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("hash password: %w", err)
	}
	return string(hashed), nil
}

// CheckPassword returns nil if plain matches the bcrypt hash.
func CheckPassword(plain, hash string) error {
	if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(hash), []byte(plain)); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid credentials")
	}
	return nil
}

// services/implementations/appointment_service_test.go
// Pattern: sqlmock + GORM + testify — success and error paths
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/services/implementations/appointment_service_test.go
package implementations_test

import (
	"errors"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"medical-sas-api/services/contracts"
	"medical-sas-api/services/implementations"

	"github.com/DATA-DOG/go-sqlmock"
	"github.com/google/uuid"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// setupTestDB creates an in-memory mock database for service tests.
// Returns the GORM DB and the sqlmock controller for expectation setup.
func setupTestDB(t *testing.T) (*gorm.DB, sqlmock.Sqlmock) {
	t.Helper()
	db, mock, err := sqlmock.New()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("failed to create mock DB: %v", err)
	}
	gormDB, err := gorm.Open(postgres.New(postgres.Config{Conn: db}), &gorm.Config{})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("failed to open GORM DB: %v", err)
	}
	t.Cleanup(func() { db.Close() })
	return gormDB, mock
}

// TestCreate_Success verifies that a valid DTO produces a non-nil UUID.
func TestAppointmentService_Create_Success(t *testing.T) {
	db, mock := setupTestDB(t)
	svc := implementations.NewAppointmentService(db)

	dto := contracts.AppointmentDTO{
		OrganizationID: uuid.New(),
		PatientID:      uuid.New(),
		DoctorID:       uuid.New(),
		DateTime:       time.Now(),
		Status:         "scheduled",
	}

	newID := uuid.New()
	mock.ExpectBegin()
	mock.ExpectQuery(`INSERT INTO "appointments" .* RETURNING "id"`).
		WithArgs(
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // created_at
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // updated_at
			nil,              // deleted_at
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // organization_id
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // patient_id
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // doctor_id
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // date_time
			"scheduled",      // status
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // id
		).
		WillReturnRows(sqlmock.NewRows([]string{"id"}).AddRow(newID))
	mock.ExpectCommit()

	gotID, err := svc.Create(dto)

	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, newID, gotID)
	assert.NoError(t, mock.ExpectationsWereMet())
}

// TestAppointmentService_Create_DBError verifies the service surfaces DB errors.
func TestAppointmentService_Create_DBError(t *testing.T) {
	db, mock := setupTestDB(t)
	svc := implementations.NewAppointmentService(db)

	dto := contracts.AppointmentDTO{
		OrganizationID: uuid.New(),
		PatientID:      uuid.New(),
		DoctorID:       uuid.New(),
		DateTime:       time.Now(),
		Status:         "scheduled",
	}

	mock.ExpectBegin()
	mock.ExpectQuery(`INSERT INTO "appointments" .* RETURNING "id"`).
		WillReturnError(errors.New("connection refused"))
	mock.ExpectRollback()

	gotID, err := svc.Create(dto)

	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, uuid.Nil, gotID)
	assert.NoError(t, mock.ExpectationsWereMet())
}

// TestAppointmentService_FindByStatus_NotFound verifies gorm.ErrRecordNotFound is surfaced.
func TestAppointmentService_FindByStatus_NotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db, mock := setupTestDB(t)
	svc := implementations.NewAppointmentService(db)

	mock.ExpectQuery(`SELECT .* FROM "appointments"`).
		WithArgs("NOTEXIST", sqlmock.AnyArg()).
		WillReturnError(gorm.ErrRecordNotFound)

	result, err := svc.FindByStatus("NOTEXIST")

	db, mock, err := sqlmock.New()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("failed to create mock DB: %v", err)

	if err != nil {

	}
	t.Cleanup(func() { db.Close() })
	return gormDB, mock
}

// TestCreate_Success verifies that a valid DTO produces a non-nil UUID.
func TestAppointmentService_Create_Success(t *testing.T) {
	db, mock := setupTestDB(t)
	svc := implementations.NewAppointmentService(db)

	dto := contracts.AppointmentDTO{
		OrganizationID: uuid.New(),
		PatientID:      uuid.New(),
		DoctorID:       uuid.New(),
		DateTime:       time.Now(),
		Status:         "scheduled",
	}

	newID := uuid.New()
	mock.ExpectBegin()
	mock.ExpectQuery(`INSERT INTO "appointments" .* RETURNING "id"`).
		WithArgs(
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // created_at
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // updated_at
			nil,              // deleted_at
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // organization_id
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // patient_id
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // doctor_id
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // date_time
			"scheduled",      // status
			sqlmock.AnyArg(), // id
		).
		WillReturnRows(sqlmock.NewRows([]string{"id"}).AddRow(newID))
	mock.ExpectCommit()

	gotID, err := svc.Create(dto)

	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, newID, gotID)
	assert.NoError(t, mock.ExpectationsWereMet())
}

		PatientID:      uuid.New(),
		DoctorID:       uuid.New(),
		DateTime:       time.Now(),
		Status:         "scheduled",
	}

	mock.ExpectBegin()
	mock.ExpectQuery(`INSERT INTO "appointments" .* RETURNING "id"`).

	mock.ExpectRollback()

	gotID, err := svc.Create(dto)

	assert.Error(t, err)

	assert.NoError(t, mock.ExpectationsWereMet())

	assert.Error(t, err)

	assert.NoError(t, mock.ExpectationsWereMet())

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestRespondWithJSON(t *testing.T) {
	ru := &utils.JSONResponseUtil{}

	tests := []struct {
		name       string
		code       int
		payload    any
		wantStatus int
		wantKey    string
	}{
		{
			name:       "200 ok with map payload",
			code:       http.StatusOK,
			payload:    map[string]string{"message": "ok"},
			wantStatus: http.StatusOK,
			wantKey:    "message",
		},
		{
			name:       "201 created",
			code:       http.StatusCreated,
			payload:    map[string]string{"id": "abc-123"},
			wantStatus: http.StatusCreated,
			wantKey:    "id",
		},
		{
			name:       "400 bad request",
			code:       http.StatusBadRequest,
			payload:    map[string]string{"error": "invalid input"},
			wantStatus: http.StatusBadRequest,
			wantKey:    "error",
		},
	}

	for _, tc := range tests {
		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			app := fiber.New()
			app.Get("/test", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
				return ru.RespondWithJSON(c, tc.code, tc.payload)
			})

			req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/test", nil)
			resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
			require.NoError(t, err)
			require.Equal(t, tc.wantStatus, resp.StatusCode)
			require.Equal(t, "application/json", resp.Header.Get("Content-Type"))

			var body map[string]any
			require.NoError(t, json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&body))
			_, ok := body[tc.wantKey]
			require.True(t, ok, "expected key %q in response", tc.wantKey)
		})
	}
}

<go_pkg> dockerfile

# Dockerfile — multi-stage build for a Go Fiber application
# Pattern: golang builder + debian:bookworm-slim runtime
# Observed in: Medical-App-Core/Dockerfile

# ─── Stage 1: Build ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM golang:1.21 AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Copy dependency manifests first for better layer caching.
# Only re-downloads modules when go.mod or go.sum changes.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

# Copy source and build the binary from cmd/
COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o app ./cmd

# ─── Stage 2: Runtime ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM debian:bookworm-slim

WORKDIR /app

# CA certificates are required for outbound HTTPS (e.g., Stripe API).
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates curl && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=builder /app/app .

EXPOSE 8080

# Document all required environment variables (values supplied at runtime).
ENV DB_HOST="" \
    DB_PORT="5432" \
    DB_USER="" \
    DB_PASSWORD="" \
    DB_NAME="" \
    DB_TIMEZONE="UTC" \
    JWT_SECRET="" \
    RABBITMQ_BASE_URL="" \
    SWAGGER_USER="" \
    SWAGGER_PASSWORD="" \
    PORT="8080"

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

COPY ./docker/api/entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh /app/app

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
</go_file>

<go_pkg> docker-compose

# docker-compose.yml — local development environment
# Pattern: app + postgres + rabbitmq services with health checks
# Observed from: Medical-App-Core infrastructure patterns
version: "3.9"

services:
  api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      - DB_HOST=postgres
      - DB_PORT=5432
      - DB_USER=postgres
      - DB_PASSWORD=postgres
      - DB_NAME=appdb
      - DB_TIMEZONE=UTC
      - JWT_SECRET=local-dev-secret-change-in-prod
      - RABBITMQ_BASE_URL=amqp://guest:guest@rabbitmq:5672/
      - SWAGGER_USER=admin
      - SWAGGER_PASSWORD=admin
      - PORT=8080
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
      POSTGRES_DB: appdb
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pg_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  rabbitmq:
    image: rabbitmq:3.13-management-alpine
    ports:
      - "5672:5672"
      - "15672:15672"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "rabbitmq-diagnostics", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  pg_data:
</go_file>

<go_pkg> shell

#!/bin/sh
# docker/api/entrypoint.sh
# Pattern: validate required env vars before exec'ing the binary
# Observed in: Medical-App-Core/docker/api/entrypoint.sh
set -e

echo "Checking required environment variables..."

MISSING=0
for VAR in DB_HOST DB_PORT DB_USER DB_PASSWORD DB_NAME JWT_SECRET; do
  if [ -z "$(eval echo \$${VAR})" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $VAR is not set"
    MISSING=1
  else
    echo "OK: $VAR is set"
  fi
done

if [ "$MISSING" -eq 1 ]; then
  echo "One or more required environment variables are missing. Aborting."
  exit 1
fi

echo "All required variables present. Starting application..."
exec ./app
</go_file>

<go_version> go1.21
<go_pkg> jenkins

// Jenkinsfile — CI/CD pipeline
// Pattern: multi-stage pipeline: test → build → docker → deploy
// Observed in: Medical-App-Core/Jenkinsfile
pipeline {
    agent any

    environment {
        DOCKER_IMAGE = "myorg/my-api"
        DOCKER_TAG   = "${BUILD_NUMBER}"
        GO_VERSION   = "1.21"
    }

    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                checkout scm
            }
        }

        stage('Test') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    go test ./... -v -coverprofile=coverage.out
                    go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html
                '''
            }
            post {
                always {
                    publishHTML(target: [
                        reportName:  'Go Coverage',
                        reportDir:   '.',
                        reportFiles: 'coverage.html',
                    ])
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Lint') {
            steps {
                sh 'go vet ./...'
            }
        }

        stage('Build Binary') {
            steps {
                sh 'CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o app ./cmd'
            }
        }

        stage('Docker Build') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG} .'
                sh 'docker tag ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG} ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:latest'
            }
        }

        stage('Docker Push') {
            steps {
                withCredentials([usernamePassword(
                    credentialsId: 'dockerhub-creds',
                    usernameVariable: 'DOCKER_USER',
                    passwordVariable: 'DOCKER_PASS'
                )]) {
                    sh '''
                        echo "$DOCKER_PASS" | docker login -u "$DOCKER_USER" --password-stdin
                        docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG}
                        docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:latest
                    '''
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            sh 'docker system prune -f || true'
        }
        failure {
            echo "Pipeline failed — check logs above"
        }
    }
}
</go_file>

                        reportName:  'Go Coverage',
                        reportDir:   '.',
                        reportFiles: 'coverage.html',
                    ])
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Lint') {
            steps {
                sh 'go vet ./...'
            }
        }

        stage('Build Binary') {
            steps {
                sh 'CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o app ./cmd'
            }
        }

        stage('Docker Build') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG} .'
                sh 'docker tag ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG} ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:latest'
            }
        }

        stage('Docker Push') {
            steps {
                withCredentials([usernamePassword(
                    credentialsId: 'dockerhub-creds',
                    usernameVariable: 'DOCKER_USER',
                    passwordVariable: 'DOCKER_PASS'
                )]) {
                    sh '''
                        echo "$DOCKER_PASS" | docker login -u "$DOCKER_USER" --password-stdin
                        docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG}
                        docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:latest
                    '''
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            sh 'docker system prune -f || true'
        }
        failure {
            echo "Pipeline failed — check logs above"
        }
    }
}
